subject: Statistics
title: Fractional Factorials
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Fractional Factorials.

hook.text: A full factorial with 7 factors at 2 levels needs 128 experimental runs. A fraction needs just 16.

buildup.text: Fractional factorials test only a carefully chosen subset of all possible combinations.

discovery.text: The trick: sacrifice the ability to estimate high-order interactions, which are usually negligible, to save runs.

twist.text: Aliasing means some effects are confounded with each other. You can't tell them apart. Choose your fraction wisely.

climax.text: In industry, fractional factorials screen dozens of factors quickly to find the few that matter most.

punchline.text: Test a fraction, learn most of the story. Efficiency over completeness.

quiz.question: What do fractional factorial designs sacrifice to reduce the number of experimental runs?
options: The ability to estimate high-order interactions, which are usually negligible
The ability to detect any main effects
